info.title: Anaplan Administration API
info.description:
Bulk user management for an Anaplan tenant. Supports importing users from a CSV file and exporting all users to CSV.

Authentication: `AnaplanAuthToken` header, using a token obtained from the Authentication API or an OAuth Authorization Code access token.

servers.description: Production (US1, US2, US5, US7, EU1, EU2, EU4, AP1 and other legacy regions sharing the non-prefixed host)

get.description:
Minimum role: Tenant Administrator.

Downloads all users in the tenant as a CSV file. Columns: `username`, `first_name`, `last_name`, `licenses`. Use `Limit` and `Offset` to page through large tenants.

parameters.name: Limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of rows to return.

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Number of rows to skip before returning results.

200.description: CSV file containing user records. Columns: `username`, `first_name`, `last_name`, `licenses`.

403.description: Caller lacks the required administrator role.

put.description:
Minimum role: Tenant Administrator.

Bulk-creates or updates users by uploading a CSV file. Users are matched by `username` (email address); existing users are updated, new addresses are created.

CSV columns: `username`, `first_name`, `last_name`, `licenses`. Constraints: at least one data row, no more than 500 rows, no duplicate email addresses, no empty cells. Licenses must be quoted and comma-separated when a user holds more than one.

Returns 207 Multi-Status with per-row counts and line-level errors.

file.description: CSV file containing user records to import.

207.description: Import processed. Contains per-row counts and any line-level validation errors.

400.description: Request rejected before processing. Possible causes: missing file, empty CSV, more than 500 rows, duplicate email addresses, or empty cells.

403.description: Caller lacks the required administrator role.

AnaplanAuthToken.name: Authorization
AnaplanAuthToken.description: Anaplan token authentication. Header value format: `AnaplanAuthToken <token>`. Accepted tokens: Authentication API token (username/password or CA certificate), or an OAuth Authorization Code access token using the `AnaplanAuthToken` prefix.
